name: Carrot Cake with Cream Cheese Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine sugar, eggs, and oil. Mix well.
3. Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt together in a separate bowl.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the sugar-egg mixture, stirring until just combined.
5. Stir in grated carrots, crushed pineapple, and chopped walnuts.
6. Divide batter evenly among three greased 9-inch cake pans (about 1-1/2 cups per pan).
7. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Remove cakes from pans and let cool on wire racks.
Frosting:
1. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and softened margarine until smooth.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar and vanilla, mixing until light and fluffy.
3. Once cakes are completely cooled, frost the top of each layer with cream cheese frosting.
4. Spread about 3/4 cup frosting between layers and on top of the last layer.
5. Do not frost the sides of the cake.
